mappings.rationaleAI: Consumer choice addresses scarcity and value (A) and consumer behaviour (B.1). Market structures address competitive markets (C.1.a, C.1.b). Price mechanisms address the price system (D.1).
Resource allocation addresses scarcity (A). Market mechanisms address markets (C.1). Public goods address price system limitations (D.2).
Cognitive biases address consumer tastes (B.2). Imperfect knowledge addresses buyer limitations (D.2.d).
Utility theory addresses scarcity and value (A) and consumer behaviour (B.1).
Monopoly regulation addresses competitive markets (C.1.a), government regulation (C.1.d), and price-fixing (D.2.a).
Strategic decision-making addresses seller behaviour (C.1.b).
Externalities address economic relationships not susceptible to prices (D.2.c).
Advertising strategy addresses consumer tastes (B.2).
Market analysis addresses consumer spending (B.2). Ethics addresses consumer protection (B.3).

mappings.vsiAuthor: John O. S. Wilson, John Goddard
mappings.rationaleAI: Banking addresses money and capital markets (C.2.c).
Asset valuation addresses money and capital markets (C.2.c).
The invisible hand addresses market theory (C.4) and the price system (D.1).
Market development addresses market history (C.4) and price system (D.1).
Collective ownership addresses markets under socialism (C.3).
WTO addresses international trade markets (C.5).
Tax instruments address government price controls (D.2.b).

Covers how brands function psychologically and culturally including consumer empowerment, data-driven marketing, and brand loyalty, directly relevant to factors influencing consumers' tastes and spending and markets for manufactured goods.

Covers comparative analysis of global economic development including the roles of trade, institutional frameworks, and government policy, directly relevant to the historical development of markets and the market in economic theory.

Covers entrepreneurial processes and their impact on market economies including venture creation and innovation across commercial sectors, relevant to competition and monopoly in markets and markets for manufactured goods.
Covers how enterprises address their societal impacts including stakeholder engagement, transparency, and labor practices, relevant to the protection of consumer interests.

Covers legal frameworks governing ownership of creative works and inventions including conflicts between creators and consumers, relevant to government regulation of monopolistic practices through patent and trademark law.
Covers the EU single market, trade policy, and regulatory mechanisms ensuring competition, directly relevant to government regulation of monopolistic practices in international contexts.
